label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's see. Jane quit her job on March 20, 2020. 176 days have passed since then. I need to find the date tomorrow in MM/DD/YYYY format. First, I need to figure out what date is 176 days after March 20, 2020. Then add one day to that to get tomorrow's date. Let me start by adding the days month by month. March has 31 days. Since March 20 is the starting point, there are 31 - 20 = 11 days remaining in March. So after March 20, there are 11 days left in March. Let me subtract those 11 days from the total 176 days. That leaves 176 - 11 = 165 days. Next, April has 30 days. Subtracting April's days: 165 - 30 = 135 days left. May has 31 days. Subtracting May: 135 - 31 = 104 days left. June has 30 days. Subtracting June: 104 - 30 = 74 days left. July has 31 days. Subtracting July: 74 - 31 = 43 days left. August has 31 days. Subtracting August: 43 - 31 = 12 days left. Now we're in September. So, 12 days after August 31, 2020 would be September 12, 2020. Wait, but if there are 12 days left, then adding those to September 1 would give us September 12, right? Because after August 31 is September 1. So adding 12 days would take us to September 12. But the question is asking for the date tomorrow, meaning one day after the 176 days. So the date 176 days after March 20, 2020 would be September 12, 2020. Then tomorrow would be September 13, 2020. So the answer would be 09/13/2020. Looking at the options, that's option A. Let me check again to make sure I didn't make a mistake in the calculation. March 20 to March 31: 11 days (March 20 + 11 days is March 31). April 30 days. May 31, June 30, July 31, August 31, and then September 12. Let me count all the days: March: 11 days April: 30 May: 31 June: 30 July: 31 August: 31 September: 12 Total: 11 + 30 +
